ministry_department: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
short_description: Comprehensive adolescent health program providing health services, counseling, and nutrition support for 10-19 years age group.
detailed_description: RKSK is a comprehensive program under National Health Mission to address the health needs of adolescents in the age group of 10-19 years. The program focuses on six key areas: nutrition, sexual and reproductive health, mental health, injuries and violence, substance misuse, and non-communicable diseases. Services are provided through Adolescent Friendly Health Clinics (AFHCs) at health facilities, peer educators, and school health programs.
key_benefits: Free health checkups and counseling for adolescents
Nutrition supplementation for anemic adolescents (IFA tablets)
Mental health counseling and support services
Peer educator support in schools and communities
other_conditions: Available to all adolescents regardless of economic status
Services provided at Adolescent Friendly Health Clinics
School health programs cover enrolled students
No registration required for basic services

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Birth certificate or school records for age verification

application_steps_offline: Visit nearest Adolescent Friendly Health Clinic (AFHC) at PHC/CHC
No appointment needed - walk-in services available
Register at reception with basic details
Consult with trained counselor or doctor
Receive health checkup, counseling, or treatment as needed
Get IFA tablets if anemic (free of cost)
Follow-up visits as advised by health provider

submission_locations.address: Primary Health Center or Community Health Center
submission_locations.working_hours: 09:00 AM - 05:00 PM (specific AFHC hours may vary)
submission_locations.who_should_visit: Adolescent (can come alone or with parent)
submission_locations.notes: Confidential services available, peer educators also provide support in schools

faq.question: Are the services confidential?
faq.answer: Yes, all counseling and health services at AFHC are completely confidential.

faq.question: Can I visit AFHC without parents?
faq.answer: Yes, adolescents can visit AFHC independently for consultation and services.

faq.question: What services are provided?
faq.answer: Health checkups, nutrition counseling, mental health support, reproductive health education, substance abuse counseling, and treatment for common ailments.

tips_for_successful_application: Locate nearest AFHC through ASHA worker or school health program
Feel free to discuss any health concerns confidentially
Take IFA tablets regularly if prescribed for anemia
Participate in peer educator programs at school
